extraction.one_line_summary: DENNIS Rowan won the Stage 16 ITT, while YATES Simon extended his GC lead to 32 seconds over Valverde through unexpectedly strong time trialing.
tactical_patterns: BMC deployed dual-pronged ITT strategy with Dennis and Rosskopf both finishing top-2, suggesting team reserve power investment.
YATES Simon controlled start/finish but attacked middle section of ITT, inverting conventional pacing wisdom for GC defense.
KWIATKOVSKI crushed ITT one day after Covadonga victory, revealing time-trialing form doesn't deteriorate immediately post-mountain effort as commonly assumed.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Extended GC lead in non-preferred discipline through tactical pacing, mental focus overcame technical weakness
rider_signals.KWIATKOVSKI Michal: negative: Strong ITT performance but suggests cumulative fatigue pattern where post-mountain-win efforts still extract future cost
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: neutral: Used Castroviejo as real-time benchmark split indicator, tactical approach to gauge performance mid-effort
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Disappointing ITT despite expectations suggests third-week fatigue compromising even steady-state efforts
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: negative: More disappointing than Quintana in ITT reveals climbing specialists' time trial degradation accelerates late-race
